exercises.name: Seated Machine Chest Press
exercises.focus: Primary focus on the pectoralis major, with synergistic engagement from the anterior deltoid and triceps brachii.
exercises.ins: Adjust the seat height so the handles align with the mid-chest. Maintain a neutral spine against the backrest with feet firmly planted. Press the handles forward until the elbows are nearly fully extended, avoiding joint lockout. Lower the weight under control during the eccentric phase.

exercises.name: Machine Lat Pulldown
exercises.focus: Primary engagement of the latissimus dorsi and upper back musculature.
exercises.ins: Secure your thighs under the knee pads. Grasp the bar with a pronated grip slightly wider than shoulder-width. Initiate the pull by depressing the scapulae, then drive the elbows down to bring the bar toward the upper chest. Avoid excessive lumbar extension or shrugging. Return the bar to the starting position with a controlled eccentric tempo.

exercises.name: Seated Machine Row
exercises.focus: Targets the latissimus dorsi, rhomboids, and middle-to-lower trapezius.
exercises.ins: Adjust the seat so the chest pad is at sternum level. Maintain a stabilized torso and pull the handles toward the lower chest or abdomen. Focus on maximal scapular retraction at the peak of the concentric phase. Extend the arms slowly during the eccentric phase.

exercises.name: Machine High Row
exercises.focus: Emphasizes upper back thickness, specifically targeting the posterior deltoids and rhomboids.
exercises.ins: Utilize a wide grip with the chest elevated against the pad. Drive the elbows back and outward, pulling the handles toward the upper chest. Pause briefly at peak contraction before performing a controlled eccentric return.

exercises.name: Machine Assisted Pull-Up
exercises.focus: Foundational movement for developing latissimus dorsi strength and overall vertical pulling mechanics.
exercises.ins: Select the appropriate counterbalance weight. Kneel or step onto the platform, engage the core, and elevate the chest. Initiate the pull by driving the elbows down and back. Lower your body with a controlled eccentric tempo.

exercises.name: Cable Face Pull
exercises.focus: Targets the posterior deltoids and enhances rotator cuff stability.
exercises.ins: Set the cable pulley to face level. Pull the rope attachment toward your forehead or the bridge of your nose, allowing the elbows to flare outward. Focus on scapular retraction and external rotation. Maintain tension throughout the return phase.

exercises.name: Seated Leg Press
exercises.focus: A higher foot placement emphasizes the glutes and hamstrings, while a lower placement shifts the focus to the quadriceps.
exercises.ins: Keep your lower back and tailbone securely against the backrest with feet shoulder-width apart. Begin with knees flexed at approximately 90 degrees. Extend the hips and knees to press the sled upward, avoiding knee lockout. Lower the sled under strict control.

exercises.name: Machine Leg Extension
exercises.ins: Align the knee joint with the machine's axis of rotation and position the roller pad against the lower shin. Extend the knees fully, holding the peak contraction briefly. Lower the weight slowly during the eccentric phase.

exercises.name: Machine Leg Curl
exercises.ins: Align the knee joint with the machine's pivot point. Position the roller pad against the lower calf or Achilles tendon. Flex the knees to curl the weight toward the glutes. Control the eccentric extension back to the starting position.

exercises.name: Barbell / Machine Hip Thrust
exercises.focus: Primary activation of the gluteus maximus.
exercises.ins: Support the upper back on a bench, plant feet flat on the floor with knees flexed at approximately 90 degrees. Extend the hips upward, fully contracting the glutes at the top. Avoid lumbar hyperextension by keeping the chin tucked and ribcage depressed.

exercises.name: Machine Hip Abduction
exercises.focus: Targets the gluteus medius, gluteus minimus, and the broader hip abductor complex.
exercises.ins: Sit with a stable pelvis and press the legs outward against the pads. Move within a controllable range of hip abduction. Return slowly, avoiding resting the weight plates between repetitions to maintain constant tension.

exercises.name: Machine Hip Adduction
exercises.focus: Isolation of the hip adductor complex.
exercises.ins: Position the legs on the outside of the pads. Squeeze the thighs inward toward the midline. Maintain torso stability and perform a slow, controlled eccentric return.

exercises.name: Machine Biceps Curl
exercises.focus: Targets the biceps brachii and brachialis.
exercises.ins: Align the elbows with the machine's axis of rotation and rest the upper arms flat on the pad. Flex the elbows to curl the handles upward. Execute a controlled eccentric lowering phase.

exercises.name: Cable Triceps Pushdown
exercises.focus: Targets the lateral and medial heads of the triceps brachii.
exercises.ins: Pin the elbows strictly to the sides of the torso. Extend the elbows fully, pressing the attachment downward. Pause at peak contraction, then control the return until the elbows are at a 90-degree angle.

exercises.name: Machine Abdominal Crunch
exercises.ins: Secure the chest pad against the upper torso. Brace the core, then flex the spine forward to crunch the torso. Avoid using the arms to pull the weight; initiate the movement entirely via abdominal contraction.

exercises.name: Kneeling Cable Crunch
exercises.focus: Scalable isolation of the rectus abdominis.
exercises.ins: Attach a rope to a high pulley. Kneel and hold the rope behind the neck. Keep the hips stationary and flex the spine, bringing the ribcage down toward the pelvis. Return slowly with a controlled eccentric stretch.
